response: Founded in 1861, the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) holds the #1 overall university ranking, with a student body of 11,500 taught by 1,069 faculty members and counting 98 Nobel laureates among its affiliates. Its highly selective admissions program has a mere 3.9% acceptance rate, charges an annual tuition of $57,590, and boasts a total endowment valued at $24.6 billion, cementing its status as a top-tier global educational institution.
